reasoning: Given the late October 2025 date, there are only about two months remaining in the year. Although OpenAI continues to push the frontier of AI capabilities, an official announcement of having achieved Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) carries immense legal, economic, and regulatory implications. Notably, OpenAI's partnership with Microsoft stipulates that Microsoft's licensing rights do not apply to AGI, meaning an official declaration of AGI by OpenAI's board would have massive financial consequences and potentially sever or complicate their partnership. Due to these strong disincentives, OpenAI is highly likely to avoid officially declaring any near-term model as 'AGI', even if it is highly capable. The Polymarket price of 4% accurately reflects this steep improbability. Therefore, a 3% estimate aligns well with the short time horizon remaining and the profound structural disincentives against such an announcement.
